message:
docs(readme): correct Series pseudo-code to match real struct (br-u73po / fd90.95)

fd90.95: the Series pseudo-code at lines 400-407 misrepresented the
struct two ways. Verified against current code:

- crates/fp-columnar/src/lib.rs:679 — Column is a concrete struct
  (dtype: DType, values: Vec<Scalar>, validity: ValidityMask), NOT
  generic. The 'Column<Int64>' syntax was wrong.

- crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:136 — Series actually has 5 fields:
  name (String), index (Index), column (Column),
  categorical (Option<CategoricalMetadata>), sparse (Option<SparseDType>).
  The pseudo-code only showed 2 of them.

Rewrote the block to match the real struct: replaced 'Column<Int64>'
with 'Column' + an inline comment that dtype=Int64 and values holds
the codes; added the missing name/index/sparse fields with brief
annotations.

Pure README change. No code impact.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -398,11 +398,14 @@ The asymmetric loss matrix penalizes "allow if incompatible" (100.0) far more th
 
 ```
 Series {
-    column: Column<Int64>,  // Stores integer codes (0, 1, 2, ...)
+    name: String,
+    index: Index,
+    column: Column,                  // dtype=Int64; `values` holds the integer codes (0, 1, 2, ...)
     categorical: Some(CategoricalMetadata {
-        categories: Vec<Scalar>,  // ["low", "medium", "high"]
-        ordered: bool,            // Whether categories have total ordering
-    })
+        categories: Vec<Scalar>,     // ["low", "medium", "high"]
+        ordered: bool,               // Whether categories have total ordering
+    }),
+    sparse: None,                    // Optional pandas-style sparse metadata (separate dtype layer)
 }
 ```
